name: CAPABILITY — the charged token architecture
thesis: Make the {lifetime, role, kind, operation, destination, disposition} tuple a first-class 16-byte capability token minted by a refusing authority and required by every allocation, copy, move, hand-off and retirement — so placement becomes a pure function of the capability, cross-lifetime sharing becomes an explicit capability transition with its own ledger cell, and no byte can be allocated, copied or handed off without a token that names and charges it.
domain_grammar:
## Rows split: permanent identity (root) vs domain shard (the model's hierarchy)

`res_row` today keeps rid, name, generation, payload, name_storage, dependency_storage in ONE record
allocated by `root_plan()` — which hardcodes `.lifetime = ano_res_lifetime_engine()`. A WORLD_LEVEL
domain that loads 5000 resources therefore grows the ENGINE domain forever. §1 forbids exactly this.

```c
// PERMANENT. Root-owned, non-moving 64-slot chunks (preserves res_test_row_address and the
// non-moving-row assertion at tests/anotest_resownership.c:161). 32 bytes. This is §1's
// "minimal permanent handle directory" and nothing more.
typedef struct res_ident {
    uint64_t rid;        // FNV-1a-64, offset basis A
    uint64_t rid2;       // FNV-1a-64, offset basis B
    uint32_t name_len;
    uint32_t generation;
    uint32_t owner;      // owning domain slot; 0 = unbound
    uint16_t kind;       // published res_kind — the typed-view gate
    uint8_t  flags;      // RES_IDENT_DERIVED | RES_IDENT_DUPLICATE | RES_IDENT_EXHAUSTED
    uint8_t  pad;
} res_ident;

// DOMAIN SHARD. Allocated through the OWNING domain's METADATA capability. Dies with the domain.
typedef struct res_shard_row {
    res_owned_block name_storage;        // the name TEXT lives here now
    res_owned_block dependency_storage;
    res_owned_block payload;
    uint32_t dependency_count;
    uint32_t alias_of;                   // shared-immutable source slot, 0 = none
    uint64_t content_hash;               // hot reload
    uint16_t source_kind;                // res_source_kind (DIR | PACK)
    uint16_t source_mount;               // which mount won — hot reload needs the identity, not just mtime
} res_shard_row;
```

**The identity/name hazard, solved.** `row_bind`'s collision refusal (`resources_registry.c:362-367`)
does `memcmp(row->name, logical, len)` — on a row whose payload may already be retired. Move the name
into the domain shard and that memcmp reads freed memory. So collision refusal compares
**{rid, rid2, name_len}** and never touches name text. Two independent 64-bit hashes: a false refusal
needs a simultaneous collision in both basis-A and basis-B FNV over the same length. Diagnostics that
want to *print* the colliding name may do so only while the binding is live. This is the prerequisite
that lets names legitimately live in the domain shard (which A.2 "Registry storage" requires) and die
with it.

Each domain owns `res_shard_row **shard_chunks` plus a small open-addressed slot→shard map, both
allocated through the domain's `shard` capability. **Model B's shard is per-KIND** — `plan()` sees
`RES_CAP_KIND(cap)` and routes METADATA to that kind's arena. Kind-major registry falls out of the
seam with no registry change.

## Domain capabilities minted at open

`ano_res_domain_open` mints the domain's five standing capabilities (shard / name / dep / staging /
transfer). Payload capabilities are minted per-load because the kind varies. `root_plan()`,
`root_block_alloc()` and `root_block_alloc_locked()` are **deleted**.

Control records follow: `res_pub` and `res_retired` stop being bare `mi_malloc`
(`resources_registry.c:496`, `:519`) and come from the owning domain's METADATA capability. After
this, `mi_heap_destroy(backing.root)` genuinely reclaims a domain — today it does not, because every
payload over 1 MiB is `mi_malloc_aligned` on the *calling thread's default heap*
(`resources_registry.c:163`), i.e. the biggest blocks were never in the heap C/E claims to destroy.

## Lifetimes

`ANO_RES_LIFETIME_SHARED_IMMUTABLE` is appended **after** `TOOL_IMPORT` (the bound check at
`resources_registry.c:601-602` is `kind > ANO_RES_LIFETIME_TOOL_IMPORT`; inserting anywhere else
silently breaks `domain_open`). It is created by `ano_res_init` beside the engine domain.

## The silent cross-lifetime alias — the top correctness bug — closed

`ano_res_get` (`resources_registry.c:775-779`) returns the existing handle whenever
`row->payload.data != NULL` **without comparing** `row->payload.plan.lifetime` to the requested
lifetime. It is latent only because production loads everything into the immortal ENGINE domain.
The moment `ano_reslevel_open` exists, level B holds handles into level A's `mi_heap`, and
`ano_res_domain_retire(A)` calls `mi_heap_destroy` on them.

New hit path:

```c
res_ident *id = ident_at(slot);
if (id->owner != 0) {
    if (id->owner == lifetime.owner && shard(id)->payload.data)
        return retain(id);                              // same domain: RETAIN, single-copy, unchanged
    if (domain_kind(id->owner) == ANO_RES_LIFETIME_SHARED_IMMUTABLE)
        return alias(lifetime, id);                     // record an alias EDGE in the requester's shard
    ano_log(ANO_ERROR, "resources: '%s' is resident in domain %u; use ano_res_get_shared()",
            logical, id->owner);
    return none;                                        // REFUSE. Never alias silently.
}
```

Public surface, additive — `ano_res_get`'s signature and every existing consumer are untouched:

```c
typedef enum ano_res_share {
    ANO_RES_SHARE_REFUSE = 0,   // what plain ano_res_get does
    ANO_RES_SHARE_PROMOTE,      // move the payload into `lifetime` (or into the shared domain)
    ANO_RES_SHARE_DUPLICATE,    // an independent payload keyed "<logical>@<owner>"
    ANO_RES_SHARE_ALIAS,        // legal only when the resident owner is SHARED_IMMUTABLE
} ano_res_share;

anores_t ano_res_get_shared(ano_res_lifetime, const char *logical, ano_res_share);
int      ano_res_promote  (ano_res_lifetime from, ano_res_lifetime to, anores_t, anores_t *out);
int      ano_res_duplicate(ano_res_lifetime from, ano_res_lifetime to, anores_t, anores_t *out);
ano_res_lifetime ano_res_lifetime_shared(void);
```

All three are one implementation: mint `to = res_cap_transition(from_cap, to_lt, disposition)`
(the authority refuses illegal pairs), `res_owned_move(&old, to, &new)` (zero-copy when both sites are
transferable and share a reclaim root — otherwise exactly one copy, charged), rewrite the ident's
owner, move the shard row into the destination domain's metadata arena, republish with a bumped
generation, retire the old descriptor through the **existing** epoch queue.

**DUPLICATE's schema collision, resolved:** the registry is strictly one row per rid. A duplicate gets
its own logical key `"<logical>@<owner>"` minted by `res_derived_key()`, with `RES_IDENT_DUPLICATE` set
and `alias_of` recording the origin slot.

## Dependency disclosure and prefetch (readback exists at last)

`res_dependency_meta` is written by `res_graphics.c:572-591` and stored at
`resources_registry.c:964-975` and **never read by anything**. Meanwhile `ano_GltfParser.c:159-177`
re-derives the identical needed[]/srgb[] set by hand. Add:

```c
int res_registry_dependencies(const ano_res_read *, anores_t, res_dependency_meta *out, size_t cap);
res_kind res_registry_kind(const ano_res_read *, anores_t);   // typed views refuse a wrong kind
// public:
int ano_res_deps(const ano_res_read *, anores_t, ano_res_dep *out, size_t cap);
int ano_res_prefetch(ano_res_lifetime, const char *logical);  // synchronous closure, cycle refusal,
                                                              // bounded depth, per-edge accounting
```
Dependency records are **copied out** to the caller. They are never hung off `res_pub` as a pointer —
C.4 forbids putting mutable registry state on the read path.

## No silent refcounts

Aliasing a SHARED_IMMUTABLE resource records an **alias edge** in the *borrowing* domain's shard, and
increments a *reported* `alias_edges` statistic on the shared domain. `ano_res_domain_retire` on the
shared domain **refuses with -1 and one ERROR naming the borrowing domains** while any edge is live.
That is a named, charged, auditable, refusable edge — not a hidden refcount on a resource, and not a
free that "just happens" when a counter hits zero. I concede below that a hostile reader will call it
a refcount wearing a nicer hat.

## The derived-key type-confusion hole, closed by the naming authority

`'#'` is a legal logical-path byte (`resources_core.c:107-109` rejects only `<0x20`, `0x7f`, `\`, `:`),
so today `ano_res_get(lt, "models/viking_room.gltf#gfx")` hits the same registry slot as the
conditioned scene and `ano_res_bytes` hands the **scene block back as raw file bytes** — no kind is
checked. Fix in the grammar, not with a check: `'#'` and `'@'` become **reserved bytes**, refused by
the public `res_path_validate`. Derived and duplicate keys are constructed only by
`res_derived_key(src, suffix, out)` through a separate internal validator that permits exactly one
reserved byte, and the bound ident carries `RES_IDENT_DERIVED`. `ano_res_get` refuses to resolve a
DERIVED ident from the filesystem. Type confusion becomes unrepresentable rather than caught.
consume_model:
## Three consume classes, three capabilities, no accidental fourth

**BORROW** — `ano_res_bytes(read, h)` and every typed view (`ano_resgfx_scene`, `anoresaudio_pcm`,
`anoresscript_program`, `anoreslevel_view`). Immutable manager memory, alive only inside a registered
read scope. Unchanged from today. Typed views gain **one** new gate: `res_registry_kind()` must match,
or the view returns the zeroed sentinel. This is the only thing standing between a font handle and
`ano_resgfx_scene` today (only `SCENE_MAGIC` at `res_graphics.c:611` saves it).

**RETAIN** — the renderer holds `{anores_t, ano_res_reader, ano_res_read}` for the level's life and
walks manager views directly. `ModelAsset`/`ModelMesh`/`ModelNode`/`ModelPrimitive`
(`ano_GltfParser.h:20-51`) are **deleted**, together with their `calloc` sites
(`ano_GltfParser.c:120, 126, 131, 401, 411, 418`) and the `memcpy` of nodes/children/roots
(`:405-419`) and the `ano_res_unload(lifetime, sceneH)` at `:423`. `static ModelAsset *g_assets[16]`
(`render_api.c:18`) is deleted; grep-verified there is **zero** `free` for any of it today — the whole
legacy blueprint tree leaks for the process lifetime, owned by nobody.

**DESTRUCTIVE TRANSFER** — `ano_res_release` withdraws publication, waits for every pre-existing read
scope to cross its grace point, then hands out the block. It is zero-copy **iff**
`site.flags & RES_SITE_TRANSFERABLE`. Today that is `transfer_compatible && !pooled`, i.e. an accident
of the 1 MiB threshold; under the capability it is the model's declared answer.

The transfer contradiction is real and I resolve it explicitly rather than discovering it as a
double-free: a block an external consumer frees must not ALSO be reclaimed by
`mi_heap_destroy(backing.root)`. **Decision: `RES_ARENA_TRANSFER` is rooted in `backing.transfer_root`,
a separate `mi_heap_t` that outlives `root`.** `ano_res_release` charges `transfers_outstanding += 1`;
the consumer must call

```c
int ano_res_transfer_return(ano_res_lifetime, void *p, size_t size);  // frees AND charges -1
```

`ano_res_domain_retire` destroys `root` immediately and **queues** `transfer_root`, destroyed by the
next `ano_res_collect()` after the last transfer returns. mimalloc routes `mi_free` home from any
thread, so a foreign consumer freeing a `transfer_root` block is legal — the only rule is that
`transfer_root` must not die first. Every `ano_aligned_free` of manager memory in the tree
(`ano_GltfParser.c:200`, `anotest_resgfx.c:271`, `:310`) becomes `ano_res_transfer_return`, and a grep
gate proves none remains.

## Decoded pixels become manager-owned

`res_graphics.c:663` calls `stbi_load_from_memory`, which allocates through this TU's `malloc`
(default mimalloc heap) — in no domain, poolable by nothing. `res_registry_external_allocation`
(`resources_registry.c:242-253`) then bumps `requested_bytes`/`serving_bytes`/`allocations`/`transfers`
and **is never reversed** — the caller frees with `ano_aligned_free` and the manager is never told. So
`allocations` already exceeds `frees` by exactly the count of every texture ever decoded, and
`ano_res_stats` over-reports forever. Any bench that reads those counters today reads a number that
only goes up.

`res_graphics.c` is the only TU that defines `STB_IMAGE_IMPLEMENTATION`, so it owns the hooks:

```c
static _Thread_local res_cap g_decode_cap;   // owner-thread-only; asserted non-null in the hooks
#define STBI_MALLOC(sz)         gfx_decode_alloc(sz)
#define STBI_REALLOC(p, sz)     gfx_decode_realloc(p, sz)
#define STBI_FREE(p)            gfx_decode_free(p)
```
routed to a per-ingest **decode heap rooted in the domain's backing root** — a declared primitive of the
ingest, charged, and winking out with the domain if the ingest dies mid-way. stb's intermediates churn
there. The finished RGBA8 then goes through `res_owned_commit(&staged, home_cap, &home)` — **zero
copies when the model's staging site equals the planned home site, otherwise exactly one, charged.**
The block is adopted as a typed derived resource:

```c
anores_t ano_resgfx_image_decoded(ano_res_lifetime, const ano_res_read *, anores_t src);
// key "<source>#rgba", RES_KIND_IMAGE_DECODED, RES_ROLE_DERIVED, RES_PROVENANCE_DECODED,
// src.rid disclosed as its dependency. Served as a counted view under a read scope.
anoresgfx_pixels ano_resgfx_pixels(const ano_res_read *, anores_t decoded);
```

`res_registry_external_allocation` is **deleted**. `anoptic_res_graphics.h:193`'s comment
(`"rgba; // caller-owned: free with ano_aligned_free"`) is deleted with it. The renderer borrows the
pixels under a read scope for its GPU upload — it does not release, it does not free. The destructive
path remains, tested, for a genuine hand-off.

## The GPU binding is a resource, not a calloc

`ano_GltfParser.c:146` (`geometryPoolIndex = lodBase`) and `:241` (`materialIndex = matIdx`) write the
only two facts `ModelAsset` holds that the conditioned scene does not. New kind
`RES_KIND_GRAPHICS_BINDING`: a counted array of `{geometry_pool_index, material_index, bindless[4]}`
parallel to the scene's `prims[]`, allocated via a `RES_ROLE_DERIVED`/`RES_OP_ADOPT` capability in the
same lifetime domain, adopted under `"<source>#gpu"` with a dependency edge to `"<source>#gfx"`. The
transient per-parse arrays (`:154-157` needed/srgb/loaded/bindless, `:180` stagingBuffers, plain
calloc/free at `:218`, `:394-397`) move to the domain's STAGING capability, so every byte the parse
touches is accounted.

`parseGltf(VulkanContext*, const char *logical)` — the by-NAME asset route the spec forbids — becomes
handle-in/handle-out:

```c
anores_t ano_render_bind_scene(VulkanContext *, ano_res_lifetime, const ano_res_read *, anores_t scene);
```
No renderer entry point takes an asset name. `src/render/gltf/scratch_process.c` (an orphaned second
glTF processor that is not in `src/render/CMakeLists.txt` and does not even compile) is **deleted**,
not fixed.
streaming_pack:
## OS seam (`resources_os.h`, both platform TUs)

```c
int  rmos_read_at(rmos_file, uint64_t off, void *buf, size_t cap, size_t *got);
typedef enum rmos_advice { RMOS_ADVICE_SEQUENTIAL=1, RMOS_ADVICE_RANDOM, RMOS_ADVICE_WILLNEED } rmos_advice;
int  rmos_advise(rmos_file, uint64_t off, uint64_t len, rmos_advice);
int  rmos_stat_hint(const char *abs, uint64_t *mtime, uint64_t *size);   // ADVISORY ONLY
```
`*got == 0` is the **only** EOF signal. `0 < *got < cap` is a SHORT READ (9P/SMB do this routinely)
and the caller loops — the loop is never folded into the seam, because "read to EOF" and "deliver
exactly len" terminate differently. POSIX: `pread(2)`, EINTR retry. Win64: `ReadFile` with an
`OVERLAPPED` offset on the ordinary synchronous handle — positional and blocking, but per MSDN it
**also advances the file pointer**, so one handle is single-owner in Stage A. That goes in the header
comment or someone will assume it is thread-safe and ship a data race. `RMOS_CHUNK_MAX` (512 KiB,
already at `resources_os.h:29`) stays and becomes the chunk-pool block size; no second constant.

## `src/resources/resources_stream.c` — the chunk pool's first production consumer

`ano_mem_pool` has **zero** production consumers today (`git grep ano_mem_pool_ -- src` returns only
`pools.c`). Under the capability it is not a special case: it is `RES_ARENA_CHUNK`, reached by a
capability with `RES_DEST_CHUNK`. A model that offers a bounded pool serves it; a model that does not
routes to its payload arena — and the bench sees the difference. That is the roster rule satisfied
without a synthetic consumer.

```c
int  res_read_range(const char *abs, uint64_t off, size_t len, void *dst); // exactly len or fail
int  res_hash_file (const char *abs, uint64_t *out_hash, uint64_t *out_size); // 512 KiB at a time
void *res_chunk_acquire(void); void res_chunk_release(void *);
```
`res_read_range` returns **three** outcomes: `0` = exactly len; `RES_RANGE_EOF` = the file ended first
(a caller error, not an IO error); `-1`/`-2` = open/mid-file failure, matching `res_read_all`'s
existing convention that the mount walk depends on (`-1` → try the next root, `-2` → stop). A silent
short return is the exact byte-truth violation A.2 forbids. Chunk-pool exhaustion degrades to one
charged `mi_malloc_aligned` chunk — never blocks, never fails the read — and the test forces it with
`max_blocks = 1`.

Three genuine consumers, so the pool is not scaffolding: codec staging; `res_hash_file` (hot-reload
confirmation, builder payload hashes, TOC hashing, all with no full-payload allocation); and the
builder's streaming payload copy. A raw ranged read needs **no** chunk — `rmos_read_at` writes into
`dst` directly, and I refuse to fake a copy to justify the pool.

Public: `int ano_res_read_range(const char *, uint64_t, size_t, void *dst);` and
`anores_t ano_res_get_range(ano_res_lifetime, const char *, uint64_t, size_t);` (keyed
`"<logical>#<off>+<len>"` through `res_derived_key`).

## Codecs

Vendor `external/lz4/` (lz4.c/h + lz4hc.c/h, BSD-2, four files — fits "no heavyweight deps"); hand-write
its 6-line `CMakeLists.txt` mirroring the jsmn/stb pull-in at `CMakeLists.txt:127-141`. zstd behind
`option(ANOPTIC_ZSTD "Vendor the zstd bulk codec" OFF)`.

`src/resources/codec/res_codec.{h,c}` — module-private, so no codec symbol escapes `src/resources/`
(the same law that keeps cgltf/stb inside `graphics/`).

Decode is **`LZ4_decompress_safe` only** — never `LZ4_decompress_fast`, which on untrusted pack bytes
is a remote memory-corruption primitive — and the produced length must equal `dst_len` **exactly**.

Framing arithmetic, and this is the trap: a compressed entry is a sequence of `u32 csize_le` + csize
bytes, each decoding to exactly **`RES_CODEC_CHUNK = 496 KiB`** uncompressed. Not 512:
`LZ4_COMPRESSBOUND(496K) ≈ 498 KiB` fits one 512 KiB pooled chunk; `LZ4_COMPRESSBOUND(512K) ≈ 514 KiB`
does not. Store-raw bypass is a pure function of the bytes: `csize >= size - size/64` → store RAW.

**Stated Stage A non-goal:** ranged reads *into* an LZ4/zstd entry are unsupported (sequential frames
carry no random-access index). Ranged reads work on RAW entries and loose files. Already-compressed
assets (audio, PNG/JPEG) are stored RAW by builder policy, so the streaming shape is unaffected. Say
this loudly or someone ships a seek that returns frame-aligned garbage.

Mounting a pack with a codec-2 entry in a zstd-OFF build **refuses the whole mount** with one ERROR
naming the codec. A pack you cannot fully read is a corrupt pack.

## anopak

The frozen format from `real.md §9`, verbatim, with its three holes closed additively. 32-byte
little-endian header serialized **field-wise** with the existing `put16/put32/put64` helpers
(`resources_core.c:729-734`) — never `fwrite` of a struct. 48-byte TOC entries, strictly ascending by
rid. **`payload_hash` covers the UNCOMPRESSED bytes** — then packed-vs-loose equivalence is one hash,
and it catches decoder bugs a stored-bytes hash cannot. TOC hash always verified at mount; payload
verification always on in the builder, on by default in Debug, `ANO_RES_PACK_VERIFY` in Release. The
name table rides as an ordinary entry under the reserved logical `"__anopak/names"`, so the header
stays literally frozen.

Mount refuses (never half-mounts) on: bad magic/version; `entry_count > 1<<20`;
`toc_offset + entry_count*48 > file size`; TOC hash mismatch; rids not strictly ascending (breaks
bsearch); any offset not 4096-aligned; any `offset + csize > file size`; any unknown/unavailable codec;
any `hash_id != 1`.

**No mmap** (SIGBUS over 9P/SMB). **No cached file handle** — entry reads open a fresh handle
(`resources_os.h:12` makes fresh-handle-per-read the remote-FS floor doctrine). A squad *will* try to
"optimize" this; reject it until Stage C has queue-depth evidence.

The TOC array is allocated through the ROOT domain's `shard` capability, so pack metadata is charged
to the ownership hierarchy rather than a stray `mi_malloc`.

## Loose-over-pack shadowing, structural

`res_candidates` (`resources_core.c:230`) emits `ano_fspath` only — it cannot name a pack entry. And
the ordering law is **not** automatic: today's table is write-root > mounts-newest-first > base, so a
registered pack mount would **shadow** the loose base directory — the exact inverse of the requirement.

```c
typedef struct res_source { res_source_kind kind; ano_fspath path; const res_pack *pack; uint32_t entry; } res_source;
int res_candidates_ex(const char *logical, size_t len, res_source *out, int cap);
int res_source_read_all(const res_source *, res_cap home, res_owned_block *out);
```
**Two-pass walk**: pass 1 emits every DIRECTORY candidate (write root, dir mounts newest-first, base);
pass 2 emits every PACK candidate. Loose-shadows-pack becomes an invariant of the walk, not of
registration order. `res_candidates()` survives unchanged as the DIR-only filter for
`ano_res_resolve`/`ano_res_exists` (a packed asset resolves empty, by design).

`gfx_slurp` (`res_graphics.c:110-125`) is the silent trap: it calls `res_candidates` directly, so a
packed `.gltf`'s sibling `.bin` would fail to resolve while the `.gltf` loaded. It migrates onto
`res_source_read_all` in the same change. Open-failure-skips vs read-failure-stops semantics are
preserved exactly: a corrupt loose file still shadows a good packed copy — correct, and it matches
`res_graphics.c:121-122`.

## Bake — SCENE_VERSION 2

The block is already bake-shaped (`res_graphics.c:36-51`: `scene_hdr`, magic `RGFX`, nine u64 offsets,
16-aligned arrays, memset-0 before writing so padding is deterministic). It is **not safe to load from
disk**, and this is the sharpest hole in the whole ledger: `ano_resgfx_scene` (`:607-650`) bounds-checks
array EXTENTS (`SCENE_ARR_OK`) and validates **nothing inside them**. `prim.material` indexes
`materials[]`; `node.mesh` indexes `meshes[]`; `node.parent`, `child_first+child_count`, `children[i]`,
`roots[i]` index `nodes[]`; `indices[]` index `vertices[]`. Safe today only because cgltf constructs
them. The bake turns every one of them into an out-of-bounds read primitive handed straight to the
renderer.

Header grows two u64:
- `layout_id` — compile-time FNV over `sizeof`/`alignof` of every struct in the block. A baked block is
  a POD image of C structs; struct layout is now an ABI. Mismatch = clean refusal instead of a silent
  miscompile-grade misread. Backed by `static_assert`s freezing each struct.
- `block_hash` — FNV-1a-64 over the whole block with this field zeroed.

**One** validation pass, hoisted to load/adopt time (never per-view; a view is on the hot path):
extents, then every cross-reference above, then `block_hash`. Refuse the whole block, one log line,
sentinel out.

Aliasing, resolved so nobody re-litigates it: the block is allocated storage with no declared type, so
typed access through `anoresgfx_vertex*` is legal under C23 6.5p7, and 16-aligned offsets over a
cache-line-aligned base satisfy alignment. The bare casts at `res_graphics.c:630-646` may stand.

`ano_resgfx_model` asks the **namespace first** for the derived `"<src>#gfx"` rid. If the walk (pack or
loose) has it: gulp + validate + adopt into the same domain, same `anores_t` out, **cgltf never
touched**. Only on absence does it fall back to the cgltf ingest. `res_gfx_parse_count` is the only
honest oracle for "zero runtime parse work" and must read 0 after loading a baked scene. Dependency
disclosure survives the bake: the deps array is rebuilt from the baked block's `images[]` at load, so
prefetch behaves identically on both paths.

**Sequencing:** the bake format is frozen **last**, after skins/animations, embedded images, and the
material/sampler/uv-set completion — or `scene_hdr` churns and every baked artifact is invalidated.
Resist starting it early just because the block is already offset-shaped.

## Builder + install

`ano_res_pack_build(src_dir, out_pack)` compiled into `anoptic_core` (so tests drive it in-process and
no offline tool reaches into `resources_internal.h` from outside the module); `tools/anopak/main.c` is
a ~40-line argv wrapper. Determinism is the merge bar, proven by building twice and `memcmp`:
bytewise-sorted input walk (`rmos_scan_dir` order is explicitly unspecified); entries sorted by rid; an
rid collision is a **hard error naming both paths** (the runtime bsearch assumes uniqueness, and the
registry's collision refusal fires too late); payloads in rid order at 4096-aligned offsets with
zero-filled padding; codec choice a pure function of extension policy + the store-raw rule; **no
timestamps anywhere**; `saves/` excluded. The builder decodes every entry back and verifies
`payload_hash` before the pack is renamed into place, and writes the pack through `res_write_protocol`
so a killed builder never leaves a half-pack that mounts.

`anoptic_pack` custom target runs `anopak` over the staged `${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/resources` tree and
bakes each `.gltf` into the entry `"<logical>#gfx"`; `install(FILES ... resources.anopak)`. Engine
mounts it right after `ano_res_init`.

**State this or a squad will "fix" it:** in a Debug/dev build the source `resources/` dir mount
shadows the pack **by design**, so cgltf still runs and the zero-parse counter is nonzero. The
zero-runtime-parse bar is measured on the **installed** tree only. Do not weaken the shadowing to make
a bench go green.

## Hot reload

`republish_locked` reuses the **existing** epoch machinery (`queue_retire_locked:424`, `reader_safe:409`,
`collect_locked:450`) — no second reclamation scheme. It does **one release-store of the new pub over
the old**, never NULL in between: `ano_res_release`'s store-NULL-then-restore-on-stall
(`resources_registry.c:854-862`) would let a reader acquire a sentinel for a fully-live resource,
breaking C.10's "old-complete or new-complete, never a spurious miss".

Three steps, and step 2 is the point:
1. **CANDIDATE** (cheap, may lie): re-run `res_candidates_ex` and compare the **winning source
   identity** against the one the shard recorded, plus `rmos_stat_hint`'s (mtime, size). A newly
   appeared loose file over a packed entry is a candidate even though the row's source is the pack —
   that is the case a squad will miss by only diffing mtime on the recorded path.
2. **CONFIRM** (authoritative): `res_hash_file` vs `shard->content_hash`. mtime lies on 9P/SMB;
   the content hash decides. This also kills the reverse false positive: a touched-but-identical file
   must **not** reload.
3. **PUBLISH**: load out of place into the SAME lifetime domain, validate fully, `republish_locked`.
   A reader inside an open scope keeps seeing old-complete bytes; the old block is freed only after its
   retire epoch passes (ASan proves no UAF).

Generation contract the renderer must be told: a reload **bumps** the generation, so handles held
across a reload resolve to the sentinel. The alternative (stable gen, swapped data) hands a reader a
pointer whose bytes change underneath it. `ano_res_reload_poll` returns the changed rids and consumers
re-get.

**Derived cascade**, the bug that makes hot reload *lie*: dependency metadata records parent→children,
never source→derived. Reloading `models/x.gltf` therefore leaves the stale `models/x.gltf#gfx` scene
published and serving old geometry forever. On a confirmed reload of rid R, scan bound shards for any
whose dependency array contains R and cascade. A bounded 4096-slot scan at a 500 ms poll is free; a
reverse index is a Stage-C optimization, not a Stage-A requirement.

wounds: The capability is a 16-byte value threaded through every internal allocation, copy, move and free. Every ingest site gains a mint, and mint can REFUSE — so failure paths multiply across the module. That verbosity tax is real, it is the price of auditability, and a reviewer who wants a terser allocator interface is not wrong on ergonomics.

The charge cube is interned with a bounded table (RES_CHARGE_MAX = 256 cells, 16 KiB). The naive (kind x lifetime x role x op) product is 16384 cells and would wreck the alloc-path cache, so I intern lazily and dump overflow into cell 0. A pathological tuple explosion degrades attribution granularity to 'overflow' — counted and visible, never silent, but degraded. If the realized tuple space ever exceeds 256, the contest reads a coarser cube than it asked for.

The shared-immutable alias edge is a refcount wearing a nicer hat, and I will not pretend otherwise. It is named, charged, per-borrower, stored in the BORROWING domain's shard, reported in ano_res_stats().alias_edges, and it makes retirement REFUSE with an ERROR naming the borrowers rather than silently freeing at zero. That is materially different from a hidden counter that frees behind your back. It is still a count. A.4 bullet 7 says 'without silently adding reference counts'; I have removed the 'silently', not the counting.

transfer_root survives the domain wink. A domain retired with an outstanding transfer costs TWO mi_heap_destroys separated by a collect, so Model C/E's marquee retire-latency number must be reported both ways (clean-retire and transfer-outstanding). And ano_res_transfer_return is a new obligation on every destructive consumer — a foreign module that forgets it pins transfer_root for the process. The failure mode is retained memory, not corruption, and it is visible in transfers_outstanding — but it is an API burden I am imposing on consumers.

Stage A is owner-thread-synchronous by law. ano_reslevel_open therefore serializes glTF ingest + image decode + WAV decode + script compile for the whole level on the init thread: a multi-second hitch on Sponza. That is CORRECT for Stage A (A.4 bullet 4) and must NOT be 'fixed' with a loader thread before Stage C. I record the cold-open wall time as the baseline the ticket system will have to beat, and I expect it to look bad.

Retaining the scene block, the binding table, and the decoded pixels for the level's life is a MEASURABLE footprint regression versus today's upload-then-unload (ano_GltfParser.c:423 unloads Sponza's multi-MiB block right after GPU upload). A.2 requires the retain; I count the regression and report it rather than hiding it. It is exactly the placement wound Stage B exists to judge, and concealing it would make the contest synthetic.

Direct landing is only sound with a spill path. rmos_size_hint is a HINT by deliberate design (9P/SMB lie), and a multipool class block cannot grow in place. So 'read straight into home' = plan from the hint, read, and on a lie re-plan and pay exactly one charged copy (hint_mismatch_copies). Models that pool everything cannot direct-land small reads at all. The counter will be nonzero on the WSL /mnt 9P floor — which is a verification gate — and that number is a wound in the honest sense: it is the cost of byte-truth.

ano_mem_stripe ships in Stage A with a home bench and a wired RES_ARENA_STRIPE, but under the two TRUTHFUL scaffolds (global-pool / scoped-pool) nothing routes to it — it is reached only by Models D and E in Stage B. That is a roster item without a Stage-A production consumer, which is precisely what A.2 'Allocator roster' complains about for ano_mem_pool. I refuse to invent a fake consumer to close the box. The chunk pool DOES get a real one (res_hash_file, codec staging, builder copy); the stripe does not until D/E exist.

Reserving '#' and '@' in the public path grammar is a namespace capability the engine loses. An asset whose real filename contains '#' becomes unloadable — refused loudly, documented, but refused. I chose making type confusion unrepresentable over checking for it, and the cost is two bytes of the path alphabet.

The permanent ident directory is still a static array (RES_SLOT_MAX 4096 x 32 B). I collect peak-slot/domain/reader evidence across a full level cycle BEFORE Stage C freezes the publication scheme, but if Sponza (~70 images x2 for the derived #rgba siblings) + shaders + fonts + audio + scripts + two overlapping level domains during a crossfade exceed 4096, growing it is a publication-scheme redesign, not a constant bump. I am deferring that decision to evidence I have not yet gathered.

Model B's kind-major shard means one row's NAME can live in a different kind's arena than a related row's PAYLOAD (the source .gltf bytes are RES_KIND_BYTES; the derived '#gfx' scene is RES_KIND_GRAPHICS_SCENE). B therefore pays a cross-arena walk at level teardown and its shard locality is worse than the model's own marketing suggests. The seam supports it; it is structurally ugly, and B's benchmark will say so.

res_account_copy/res_account_transfer/res_registry_external_allocation do not check owner_thread() today — only g_reg.alive. I am adding a per-cell ledger charged with PLAIN stores on the owner-thread-only assumption (so it survives Stage C's mutex deletion without becoming a data race). Any squad that calls an accounting seam off the owner thread now gets a race that TSan will find. The gate must land WITH the ledger, not after it.

name: MINIMAL-CORE: a three-verb resource core with pluggable extensions over a placement vtable
thesis: The resource core owns exactly three things — identity (rid/slot/generation + a deliberately small permanent directory), publication (immutable descriptors + epoch reclamation + read scopes), and placement dispatch (one vtable) — and knows the word "graphics" no more than it knows the word "audio": every kind, every conditioning rule, every derived type, every dependency edge and every consume contract is declared by a registered `res_ext`, whose blocks are allocated through the same placement seam the five contest models implement, so a new resource class is one new TU and a new allocator model is one new TU, and neither touches the other or a single consumer.
domain_grammar:
## Lifetime domains

`ano_res_lifetime_kind` gains `ANO_RES_LIFETIME_SHARED_IMMUTABLE` **appended after TOOL_IMPORT** — the bound check at resources_registry.c:601-602 is `kind > ANO_RES_LIFETIME_TOOL_IMPORT`, so inserting anywhere else silently breaks `ano_res_domain_open`; the check moves to the new terminal value in the same edit. That domain never retires while a referencing domain is live; retirement is refused with `-2` and one ERROR line naming the referrers.

`RES_DOMAIN_MAX` 32 → 64 (Model B wants a domain per kind, Model E per lifetime×role); `RES_SLOT_MAX` 4096 → 16384 (Sponza alone contributes ~70 encoded images + ~70 derived `#rgba` + scene + gpu-binding, and two level domains overlap during a crossfade). Both raised against recorded peak evidence from the level-cycle test, written to `docs/benchmarks/`. `g_directory` grows to 128 KiB static. Making it *growable* is a Stage C publication-scheme change and is deliberately NOT done now.

## Registry shard ownership

Every substantial registry byte — name text, binding record, dependency array, placement metadata, payload, derived object, teardown record — is allocated by `res_shard_alloc()`, which builds a `res_place_plan` with `role = RES_ROLE_REGISTRY/NAME/DEPENDENCY`, `lifetime = the owning domain`, `kind = the binding's own kind`, and routes it through `res_placement_active()->plan()`. **The core does not choose a home for one byte of registry state.** Model A lands them in its one multipool; Model B lands each in *its kind's* metadata pool; Model C in the domain's pool; Model D in the process metadata multipool; Model E in the domain's metadata multipool. `root_plan`/`root_block_alloc`/`root_block_alloc_locked` — the three functions that today force every row, name, hash table and dep record into the ENGINE domain — are **deleted**. The only root-domain survivors are the `res_ident` chunks and the rid→slot map.

## Dependency disclosure

`res_dependency_meta{rid, kind_tag, flags}` is already produced by graphics and thrown away. It becomes readable:

- `res_registry_deps(read, h, out, cap)` (internal) and `ano_res_deps` (public) return the disclosed set from the binding's `dep_storage`.
- `res_pub` gains `kind_tag` (read at last). `dependency_count` stays a count — no pointer into mutable row storage hangs off the publication descriptor, so C.4's "reader never touches the mutable map" survives.
- `ano_res_prefetch(lt, logical)` loads the named resource, reads back its deps, and transitively resolves the closure into `lt` (or the disposition the dep's `flags` name), with a visited bitset over ident slots for cycle refusal, depth bound 8, and one accounted edge per resolution. The renderer's hand-rolled `needed[]`/`srgb[]` recomputation collapses into a gate over the declared list.
- A level's `assets` array **is** its dep array. Opening a level and prefetching a glTF's textures are literally the same mechanism.

## Cross-lifetime: no silent alias, no refcount

The live bug — `ano_res_get` returns a resident handle without comparing the resident owner to the requested lifetime — is closed at the hit path:

```
resident owner == requested        -> RETAIN: return the handle.
resident owner is SHARED_IMMUTABLE -> ALIAS:  return the handle; the requester may NOT unload it.
otherwise -> ask the extension's share_policy(kind), then res_disposition_allowed():
   REFUSE    -> sentinel + one ERROR line naming both domains
   PROMOTE   -> move the payload into `to` (zero-copy iff both sites are TRANSFERABLE and share a
                root; else exactly one charged copy), rewrite shard_owner, republish at gen+1,
                retire the old descriptor through the epoch queue
   DUPLICATE -> mint a DISTINCT identity "<logical>@<owner>": its own rid, slot, handle, binding,
                payload. One row per rid stays inviolate.
```

The DUPLICATE schema question the recon flagged as "must be decided by one person before three squads write against three assumptions" is decided here: **duplication creates a derived identity; it does not add a second payload slot to a row.** `res_disposition_allowed` becomes a *pure* predicate that mutates no counters (today it bumps `promotions`/`duplications` when a test merely asks whether a move would be legal, so every promotion number in the tree currently counts questions, not events); the real operations charge the counters.

## Kinds: a registry, not an enum

The `res_kind` enum is a guaranteed four-way merge collision (graphics wants GRAPHICS_BINDING/ANIMATION/FONT_BAKE; audio wants AUDIO_ENCODED/AUDIO_PCM; script wants SCRIPT_SOURCE/SCRIPT_BYTECODE; pack wants PACK). It dies. Extensions declare kinds by a **stable 4-byte tag** and the core interns them to a dense runtime id:

```c
typedef struct res_ext_kind {
    uint32_t    tag;        // ANO_FOURCC('R','G','F','X'): stable on disk, in packs, forever
    const char *name;       // "graphics.scene"
    bool        derived;    // a derived kind is NEVER resolvable from the filesystem
} res_ext_kind;

typedef struct res_ext {
    const char *name;                                   // "graphics"
    const res_ext_kind *kinds; size_t kind_count;
    uint32_t (*classify)(const char *logical, size_t len);            // extension owns its extensions
    int      (*derive)  (const ano_res_read *, anores_t src, ano_res_lifetime,
                         uint32_t want_tag, res_key *out_key, res_owned_block *out_block,
                         res_dependency_meta *deps, size_t cap, size_t *n);
    int      (*validate)(uint32_t tag, const void *block, size_t size);   // the HOSTILE-INPUT gate
    int      (*deps_of) (uint32_t tag, const void *block, size_t size,
                         res_dependency_meta *out, size_t cap, size_t *n);
    res_disposition (*share_policy)(uint32_t tag);
} res_ext;

int      res_ext_register(const res_ext *);      // owner thread, before res_freeze()
uint16_t res_kind_of(uint32_t tag);              // dense id, RES_KIND_MAX 32
```

`kind_from_path`'s central switch — the file every squad was told they would collide on — is replaced by a walk over registered extensions' `classify()`. Adding `.wav` touches `src/resources/audio/res_audio.c` and nothing else.

## Derived keys: a separate identity namespace

`'#'` becomes an **illegal byte** in a filesystem logical path (`byte_ok`, resources_core.c:107-109), and a derived key's rid is computed with a seeded basis:

```c
uint64_t res_rid_file   (const char *logical, size_t len);      // fnv1a64, unchanged (pack TOC)
uint64_t res_rid_derived(uint64_t src_rid, uint32_t kind_tag);  // fnv1a64_seeded(src_rid ^ tag)
```

So `"models/x.gltf#gfx"` cannot be typed as a path, cannot collide with the derived scene, and cannot be resolved from disk. `ano_res_get` refuses to bind an ident whose `derived` bit is set. `ano_res_bytes_typed` refuses a kind-tag mismatch before it dereferences a byte. The type-confusion hole is closed structurally, not by convention.
consume_model:
Three verbs, three lifetime contracts, none disguised as another.

**BORROW** — `ano_res_bytes_typed(read, h, tag)` and every extension's typed view (`ano_resgfx_scene`, `ano_resaudio_pcm`, `ano_resscript_program`, `ano_restext_bake_view`, `ano_reslevel_view`). The manager keeps the memory; the view dies at `ano_res_read_end`. Kind-checked. This is what the renderer, the audio sink, the VM and the level walker use for everything they do not consume.

**DESTRUCTIVE TRANSFER** — `ano_res_take(lifetime, h, &parcel)`. Withdraws publication, waits for every pre-existing read scope to cross its grace point (`-2` and republish if a reader is stalled, exactly as `ano_res_release` does today), then hands back a **parcel**: `{data, size, token, site}`. The recipient returns it with `ano_res_parcel_free`, which routes home through the recorded site. Zero-copy **iff** `site.flags & RES_SITE_TRANSFERABLE` — now a *placement* decision, not a caller-computed size threshold. `ano_res_parcel_zero_copy()` is the pointer-equality oracle the contest needs. Under Model A/C a sub-1 MiB take **copies** and `releases_copied` says so; under Model D/E it moves. That is precisely the thing B.6 is supposed to measure and today cannot.

The transfer/wink-out contradiction is resolved explicitly: a model promising zero-copy transfer must place transfer blocks in an arena flagged `RES_SITE_OWN_HEAP` — a heap that is *not* the domain root (D: a process transfer heap; E: a per-domain transfer sub-heap). `res_backing.outstanding_parcels` is a **teardown barrier, not a resource refcount** (it counts parcels in flight, never handle reads). `ano_res_domain_retire` returns `-2` while parcels are outstanding — the same retryable shape `ano_res_shutdown` already uses for stalled readers. A model whose transfer site *is* the domain root simply never sets `RES_SITE_TRANSFERABLE`; it copies, and the copy is counted.

**DERIVE** — `ano_res_derive(lifetime, read, src, kind_tag)` runs the owning extension's `derive()`, which allocates the conditioned block through `res_owned_alloc` (→ the active placement), discloses its dependency edges, and adopts under a derived key. Every conditioned artifact in the engine goes through this one door: scene block (`'RGFX'`), decoded pixels (`'RPIX'`), GPU binding table (`'GBND'`), PCM (`'APCM'`), bytecode (`'SBYT'`), glyph bake (`'FBAK'`), conditioned level (`'LVLC'`).

## Decoded pixels become manager-owned

`res_registry_external_allocation` — accounting-only, never reversed, so `allocations` exceeds `frees` by every texture ever decoded and `ano_res_stats` over-reports forever — is **deleted with its only caller**. The new path, inside `res_graphics.c` (the only TU where stb exists):

1. `stbi_info_from_memory` gives `w/h/comp` with no decode.
2. `res_owned_alloc(plan{kind='RPIX', role=RES_ROLE_DERIVED, destination=RES_DEST_BULK, lifetime=caller's}, w*h*4, &home)` — a real block, in a real domain, with a real site.
3. `STBI_MALLOC/REALLOC/FREE` route to the ingest's **staging arena** (`RES_ARENA_STAGING` from the same backing). stb decodes there; we `memcpy` once into `home` and charge `res_account_copy`.
4. Adopt under the derived key `<src>#rgba` with the source rid disclosed as a dep.
5. The renderer either **borrows** it under a read scope (VkBuffer staging copy) or **takes** it for a genuine hand-off. `ano_aligned_free(px.rgba)` at ano_GltfParser.c:200 and anotest_resgfx.c:271/:310 dies; a grep gate proves no `ano_aligned_free` of decoded pixels survives anywhere.

The copy in step 3 is a **declared, counted wound**, not a hidden one. A Model D/E contestant may claim it back by pointing stb's largest allocation at the home block; the copy counter is the referee.

## Renderer retains handles

`ModelAsset/ModelMesh/ModelNode/ModelPrimitive` are deleted (nothing in the tree frees them today — the whole legacy blueprint tree leaks for the process lifetime, unowned by any domain). `parseGltf(VulkanContext *, const char *logical)` — the last by-name asset route — becomes:

```c
// Handle in, handle out. No renderer entry point takes an asset name.
anores_t ano_render_bind_scene(VulkanContext *, ano_res_lifetime, const ano_res_read *,
                               anores_t scene);   // returns the 'GBND' binding handle
```

The GPU-side result of ingest — per-prim `geometry_pool_index`, `material_index`, `bindless_index[]`, the only facts `ModelAsset` held that the scene view did not — becomes a typed derived resource `'GBND'`, adopted with a dep edge back to the `'RGFX'` scene rid. The renderer retains `{sceneH, bindH, ano_res_reader, ano_res_read}` for the level's life and flattens `anoresgfx_scene` views directly. `src/render/gltf/scratch_process.c` is **deleted** (it is not in CMakeLists and does not compile; nobody should try to revive it). The transient per-parse arrays move into a `TRANSIENT_IMPORT` domain so every byte the parse touches is accounted.

Honest consequence, stated up front: retaining Sponza's scene block pins a multi-MiB block for the level's life that today is unloaded right after GPU upload. Real footprint regression, counted in `live_bytes`, reported — it is exactly the placement wound Stage B exists to judge, and hiding it would make the contest synthetic.
streaming_pack:
## Ranged reads — `rmos` grows three ops, in both platform TUs

```c
// src/resources/resources_os.h
int  rmos_read_at(rmos_file, uint64_t off, void *buf, size_t cap, size_t *got);
int  rmos_advise (rmos_file, uint64_t off, uint64_t len, rmos_advice);
int  rmos_stat_hint(const char *abs, uint64_t *mtime, uint64_t *size);   // ADVISORY ONLY
```

`*got == 0` is the **only** EOF signal; `0 < *got < cap` is a SHORT READ (9P/SMB do this routinely) and the caller loops. The loop is never folded into the seam, because "read to EOF" (`res_read_all`) and "deliver exactly len" (`res_read_range`) terminate differently. POSIX: `pread(2)`, retry EINTR. Win64: `ReadFile` with an `OVERLAPPED` carrying `Offset/OffsetHigh` on the ordinary synchronous handle — positional and blocking, but per MSDN it *also advances the file pointer*, so a handle is single-owner in Stage A. **That is written in the header**, or someone assumes thread-safety and ships a race. The signature is already the one Stage C needs. `rmos_stat_hint`'s mtime is a cheap *filter* only; a content hash decides.

## Chunk pool — `ano_mem_pool`'s first production consumer, and it is CONTESTED

`src/resources/resources_stream.c` does not own a pool. It asks the **active placement** for one:

```c
res_place_plan p = { .role = RES_ROLE_STAGING, .destination = RES_DEST_STAGING,
                     .kind = RES_KIND_BYTES, .lifetime = lt, .alignment = ANO_CACHE_LINE };
res_site site;  res_placement_active()->plan(..., &p, RMOS_CHUNK_MAX, &site);
void *chunk = res_placement_active()->alloc(..., &site, RMOS_CHUNK_MAX);
```

Model D/E route that to a bounded `ano_mem_pool(512 KiB, ANO_CACHE_LINE, max_blocks=8)` with `reserve(2)` at init; Model A routes it to its one multipool, and the bench sees the difference. `RMOS_CHUNK_MAX` (512 KiB, already defined) is the block size — no second constant. Exhaustion is false-on-empty, degrading to one charged `mi_malloc_aligned` chunk; a test with `max_blocks=1` and two nested consumers forces it.

Three genuine consumers, so the pool is not scaffolding: codec staging; `res_hash_file` (FNV-1a-64 over a whole file 512 KiB at a time with no full-payload allocation); the builder's streaming payload copy. A *raw* ranged read needs no chunk — `rmos_read_at` writes into `dst` directly — and I refuse to fake a copy to justify the pool.

```c
int res_read_range(const res_source *, uint64_t off, size_t len, void *dst);  // exactly len or fail
int res_hash_file (const res_source *, uint64_t *hash, uint64_t *size);
```

Three outcomes, three codes: `0` exactly-len; `RES_RANGE_EOF` the file ended short (a caller error, not IO); `-1/-2` open vs mid-file failure, preserving the walk's existing "-1 try the next root / -2 stop" contract. A silent short return is the byte-truth violation A.2 forbids.

Public: `ano_res_read_range(logical, off, len, dst)` and `ano_res_get_range(lifetime, logical, off, len)` — the latter keyed by a derived rid, so it serves through the same `anores_t`/read-scope grammar as any resource.

## Kill the staging copy

`ano_res_get` today reads into a default-heap buffer, then allocates the home, then memcpies. Replaced by a destination-aware sink so a read lands in its home:

```c
typedef struct res_sink {
    void *ctx;
    void *(*reserve)(void *ctx, size_t hint, size_t *out_cap);  // may hand back the HOME block
    void *(*grow)   (void *ctx, size_t need, size_t *out_cap);  // the hint LIED: spill, charge 1 copy
    void  (*commit) (void *ctx, size_t final);
} res_sink;
int res_read_sink(const res_sink *, const res_source *, size_t *out_size);
```

EOF-truth is preserved *and* direct landing works: plan a home from the hint, read into it, and on a hint-lie spill to a re-planned home with **exactly one** charged copy (`hint_mismatch_copies`). On WSL's 9P `/mnt` the hint lies and the counter is nonzero — the honest measurement, not a bug. `RES_DEST_VARIABLE_PAYLOAD`/`_BULK`/`_STAGING`/`_EXTERNAL_TRANSFER` finally mean something.

## Codecs

`external/lz4/` vendored (lz4.c/h + lz4hc.c/h, BSD-2, four files — fits "no heavyweight deps"), default ON, hand-written 6-line CMakeLists mirroring how jsmn/stb are pulled in. `external/zstd/` behind `option(ANOPTIC_ZSTD ... OFF)`. Module-private seam `src/resources/codec/res_codec.{h,c}` so no codec symbol escapes `src/resources/` — the same law that keeps cgltf/stb inside `graphics/`.

Decode is **`LZ4_decompress_safe` only** (`_fast` on untrusted pack bytes is a remote memory-corruption primitive) and must produce exactly `dst_len`; anything else is corruption. A compressed entry is a sequence of frames, each `u32 csize_le` + csize bytes, each decoding to exactly **`RES_CODEC_CHUNK = 496 KiB`** — *not* 512: `LZ4_COMPRESSBOUND(496K) ≈ 498 KiB` fits one 512 KiB pooled chunk, `LZ4_COMPRESSBOUND(512K) ≈ 514 KiB` does not. That arithmetic is the trap. Store-raw bypass is a pure function of the bytes: `csize >= size - size/64` ⇒ RAW.

**Explicit Stage A non-goal, stated in the header:** ranged reads *into* an LZ4/zstd entry are unsupported (sequential frames carry no random-access index). Already-compressed assets (audio, PNG/JPEG) are stored RAW by builder policy, so the streaming shape is unaffected. Without this stated, someone ships a seek that silently returns frame-aligned garbage.

## anopak

Format frozen exactly as `real.md` §9, with its three holes closed additively. 32-byte LE header (`"ANOPAK\0\1"`, entry_count, flags, toc_offset, toc_hash), 48-byte TOC entries **sorted strictly ascending by rid** (`rid, offset(4096-aligned), size(uncompressed), csize(stored), codec, hash_id, entry_flags, pad, payload_hash`), serialized field-wise with the existing `put16/32/64` helpers — never `fwrite` a struct. **`payload_hash` covers the UNCOMPRESSED bytes**: it is then the same oracle the loose path computes (packed-vs-loose equivalence is one hash) and it catches *decoder* bugs, which a stored-bytes hash cannot. Cost: verification requires decoding, so it is always-on in the builder, on by default in Debug, opt-in in Release; the TOC hash is always verified at mount. The name table ships as an ordinary entry under the reserved key `__anopak/names`, so the header stays literally frozen.

Mount refusal matrix — **every one of these refuses the whole mount, never half-mounts**: bad magic/version; `entry_count > 1<<20`; `toc_offset + n*48 > file size`; toc_hash mismatch; rids not strictly ascending (corruption *and* it breaks bsearch); any offset not 4096-aligned; `offset + csize > size`; any unknown or unavailable codec (a zstd-2 entry in a zstd-OFF build refuses at mount with one ERROR naming the codec — not lazily at first read, not by silently skipping the entry); any `hash_id != 1`.

**No mmap** (SIGBUS over 9P/SMB). **No cached file handle** — entry reads open a fresh handle, because fresh-handle-per-read is the remote-FS floor doctrine. A squad *will* try to "optimize" this; it is rejected until Stage C has queue-depth evidence. The TOC array is allocated through the placement seam with `role = RES_ROLE_REGISTRY`, so pack metadata is charged to the ownership hierarchy, not to a stray mi_malloc.

## Loose-over-pack: an invariant of the walk, not of registration order

Today's table is write-root > mounts newest-first > base, so a registered pack mount would **shadow** the loose base — the exact inverse of what is required. Fixed structurally:

```c
typedef struct res_source { uint8_t kind; ano_fspath path; const res_pack *pack; uint32_t entry; }
        res_source;
int res_candidates_ex(const char *logical, size_t len, res_source *out, int cap);
```

**Two passes**: pass 1 emits every DIRECTORY candidate (write root, dir mounts newest-first, base); pass 2 emits every PACK candidate (pack mounts newest-first). Loose-shadows-pack is then structurally impossible to break, regardless of mount order. `res_candidates()` survives unchanged as the DIR-only filter for `ano_res_resolve`/`ano_res_exists`. `res_source_read_all()` is the one place that dispatches DIR vs PACK; `ano_res_get`'s loop *and* `gfx_slurp` both migrate onto it — `gfx_slurp` is the silent trap, because a packed `.gltf`'s sibling `.bin` would otherwise fail to resolve while the `.gltf` loaded fine.

## Bake — and the highest-severity hole in the tree

The conditioned scene block is already load-in-place shaped and pointer-free, so the bake is not a new format: it is the same bytes on disk. But `ano_resgfx_scene` bounds-checks array **extents** and validates **nothing inside them** — `prim.material`, `node.mesh`, `node.parent`, `node.child_first/count`, `children[i]`, `roots[i]`, `indices[i]` are all unchecked, safe today only because cgltf constructed them. The moment a block arrives from a pack, a corrupt download or a hostile mod, every one of those is an out-of-bounds read primitive handed straight to the renderer.

That is why `res_ext.validate()` is **mandatory for any bakeable kind** and is called by the core at adopt time whenever `provenance == RES_PROVENANCE_PACK` (and in Debug on the derive path too, as a free oracle). `SCENE_VERSION` → 2; the header gains `uint64 layout_id` (compile-time FNV over `sizeof`/`alignof` of every struct in the block — a baked block is a POD image of C structs, so struct layout is now an ABI, and a mismatch must be a clean refusal rather than a silent miscompile-grade misread) and `uint64 block_hash`, plus `static_assert`s freezing every struct's size and alignment. Aliasing is settled and will not be re-litigated: the block is allocated storage with no declared type, so typed access is legal under C23 6.5p7; the existing bare casts stand.

`ano_resgfx_model` asks the **namespace first**: `ano_res_get(lt, derived_key)`. If the walk (pack or loose) has it → gulp + validate + adopt, cgltf never touched. Only on absence does it fall back to the cgltf ingest. A test-visible `res_gfx_parse_count` **must read 0** after loading a baked scene — prose is not evidence. Dependency disclosure survives the bake: `deps_of()` rebuilds the dep array from the baked block's `images[]`, so prefetch behaves identically on both paths.

Builder: `ano_res_pack_build(src_dir, out_pack)` compiled into `anoptic_core` (so tests drive it in-process) with a ~40-line `tools/anopak/main.c` wrapper. Determinism is the merge bar, proven by building twice and `memcmp`: bytewise-sorted input walk (`rmos_scan_dir` order is explicitly unspecified), rid-sorted entries, rid collision = hard error naming both paths and exit nonzero, payloads in rid order at 4096-aligned offsets with zero-filled padding, codec choice a pure function of extension policy + the store-raw rule, **no timestamps anywhere**, `saves/` excluded. The builder decodes back and verifies every `payload_hash` before the pack is renamed into place, and writes through `res_write_protocol` so a killed builder never leaves a half-pack that mounts.

Install: a custom target runs `anopak` over the staged `${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/resources` tree after shader/font/model staging, emitting `resources.anopak` with baked `'RGFX'` entries. `main.c` mounts it beside the existing `ANO_DEV_RESOURCES` dir mount; order does not matter, because loose-over-pack is enforced by the walk. **Stated so nobody "fixes" it: in a Debug/dev build the source `resources/` dir mount shadows the pack BY DESIGN, so cgltf still runs and the zero-parse counter is nonzero. The zero-runtime-parse bar is measured on the INSTALLED tree only.** A squad chasing a green bench will otherwise weaken the shadowing and destroy the invariant.

## Hot reload

`publish_locked` only binds an unbound row, and `ano_res_release` does store-NULL-then-restore-on-stall — a reload must **never** do that, because a reader acquiring during the NULL window sees a sentinel for a fully-live resource, breaking old-complete-or-new-complete. So:

```c
static int republish_locked(uint32_t slot, res_binding *b, res_owned_block *fresh);
// 1. build the new immutable res_pub for `fresh` at generation+1
// 2. ONE release-store of the new pub over the old. Never NULL in between.
// 3. queue_retire_locked(old_pub, old_block) at the bumped retire epoch
// 4. collect_locked() reclaims only after every lane registered before the swap is quiescent
```

It reuses the epoch machinery that already exists (`queue_retire_locked`/`reader_safe`/`collect_locked`). A second reclamation scheme is forbidden. Generation contract, and the renderer must be told: a reload BUMPS the generation, so handles held across a reload resolve to the sentinel and consumers re-get. The alternative (stable gen, swapped data) hands a reader a pointer whose bytes change underneath it.

`ano_res_reload_poll(changed, cap)`, dev-gated, frame-boundary driven, three steps. **CANDIDATE** (cheap, may lie): re-run `res_candidates_ex` and compare the *winning source identity* against the one the binding recorded, plus `rmos_stat_hint`'s (mtime,size) — a newly-appeared loose file over a packed entry is a candidate even though the binding's path is the pack, and that is the case a squad misses by only diffing mtime on the recorded path. **CONFIRM** (authoritative): `res_hash_file` against `binding->content_hash`, because mtime lies on 9P and a touched-but-byte-identical file must NOT reload. **PUBLISH**: load out of place into the same domain, run the extension's full `validate()`, then `republish_locked`. A reader inside an open scope keeps seeing old-complete bytes; the old block dies only past its retire epoch (ASan proves no UAF).

**Derived cascade** — the edge that does not exist today and whose absence makes hot reload *lie*: dep metadata records parent→children, so reloading `x.gltf` leaves the stale `'RGFX'` scene published and serving old geometry forever. On a confirmed reload of rid R, scan bound bindings for any whose `dep_storage` contains R and cascade (retire → consumers re-get, or re-ingest through the same republish path). A bounded scan of the ident table at a 500 ms poll is free; a reverse index is a Stage C optimization, not a Stage A requirement.

## Telemetry

One choke point — `res_account(op, plan, site, bytes)` — that **every** path in `src/resources/` calls. Attribution is a dense SoA cube `[kind:32][lifetime:8][role:8]` of the B.2 byte/block/copy metrics (86 KiB, root-owned, one cache line touched per allocation), plus a flat per-operation vector. New counters: `parent_calls`, `class_hits[]`, `oversize_hits`, `live_requested_bytes` (so live internal fragmentation = `live_bytes - live_requested_bytes`; today `requested/serving` are *cumulative* and their difference is a lifetime total, not fragmentation, and five existing tests already subtract snapshots of them — so these are NEW fields, not redefinitions), `external_frag_bytes`, `staging_bytes/peak`, `hint_mismatch_copies`, `releases_zero_copy`/`releases_copied`, `retire_ns`, `registry_probes`/`max_probe`/`rehashes`, `ranged_reads`, `range_bytes`, `chunk_acquires`, `chunk_pool_exhaustions`, `pack_lookups/hits/bytes_stored/bytes_served`, `codec_decodes/bytes_in/bytes_out`, `reload_candidates/confirmed/rejected_same_content`, `parse_count`. Plus `ano_res_domain_stats(lifetime, *out)` — the per-domain residual accessor B.5/B.7 are *judged on* and which today cannot even be computed (direct >1 MiB blocks contribute nothing to `chunk_bytes`, so a domain's true footprint is not derivable from the existing stats). `ano_res_stats_delta(before, after)` kills the hand-rolled subtraction in five tests. Counters are owner-thread-only from the start (the gate already exists), and `ano_res_stats()` publishes an immutable snapshot — so Stage C's mutex deletion does not turn them into a data race.

`anotest_resbench`'s `res_registry_stats()` shim currently sets `direct_bytes = s.live_bytes` *and* `pools.live_bytes = s.live_bytes` and then asserts one against the other. Every "pooled vs direct split" number it has ever printed is a self-comparison. It is rewritten against the real per-domain/per-arena accessors, and no figure from the old shim is carried into the contest.

wounds: The extension vtable puts an indirect call and a fourcc->dense-id intern on every bind and every derive. Reads never touch it (the publication descriptor carries the kind tag directly), so the hot path is safe -- but the LOAD path pays for the modularity. Small, real, and I will not pretend it is zero.

Interning kinds by fourcc means the dense runtime id depends on registration order. Packs are safe (they store fourccs on disk), but the stats cube, any memory dump, and any bench table keyed by a dense id are NOT comparable across builds if registration order changes. Mitigation is a fixed `res_ext_all[]` list -- a discipline, not a guarantee. It is a footgun and I am naming it.

Refusing to keep name TEXT in the permanent directory is what makes shard-owned names legal, but it costs a diagnostic: an rid collision whose resident binding lives in an already-retired domain can name only the INCOMING path, not the resident one. A real regression in the collision-refusal message, accepted so that A.2 'Registry storage' can actually hold.

`ano_res_take` makes `ano_res_domain_retire` retryable (-2 while parcels are outstanding). That is a NEW failure mode for every caller, and a caller who does not loop leaks a domain. The alternative -- letting mi_heap_destroy race an outstanding transfer -- is a double-free that only fires when a domain retires with a transfer in flight, so this is the right trade. It is still a trade.

Under Model A and Model C, `ano_res_take` of a sub-1 MiB block COPIES, where today a >1 MiB block moves free. Sponza's per-texture GPU hand-off therefore gets measurably slower under two of the five contestants. That is the wound the contest exists to expose -- but it IS a live regression against today's behavior, and the benchmark must report it rather than bury it.

Retaining the conditioned scene block for the level's life (required by A.2, so the level domain owns something to wink out) pins a multi-MiB direct allocation for Sponza that today is unloaded right after GPU upload. Real, measurable footprint regression. Counted in live_bytes, reported, not hidden.

Decoded pixels cost ONE copy: stb_image has no per-call allocator hook, so it decodes into the staging arena and we memcpy into the owned home. `stbi_info` plus routing stb's largest allocation to the home could eliminate it, but that is fragile enough that I refuse to ship it as the Stage A baseline. We pay the copy and count it; a Model D/E contestant may claim it back and the copy counter is the referee.

The core must support two retire shapes -- WINKS_WHOLE (skip per-block frees, destroy the heap) and retail-walk. A model that sets WINKS_WHOLE while an arena's parent is NOT the domain root double-frees or leaks silently. A Debug post-wink assertion (per-domain live_bytes must return to zero) plus ASan catches it, but the invariant is enforced by test, not by the type system.

The attribution cube is dense over (kind x lifetime x role) with a SEPARATE flat per-operation vector, not a full four-way cross. B.2 asks for all four axes. A weakness that exists only in one (kind x operation) cell -- say, promotion of audio PCM specifically -- can still hide. I chose 86 KiB and one cache line per alloc over a 3780-cell cube that would itself become the cache pathology the contest is measuring. That is a judgment call, and it is a hole.

Everything in Stage A is owner-thread-synchronous by design, so opening a level serializes glTF ingest + image decode + WAV decode + script compile on the init thread: a multi-second hitch on Sponza. That is CORRECT for Stage A (A.4 bullet 4) and must NOT be 'fixed' by sneaking in a loader thread before Stage C -- but it means the first level open is visibly, embarrassingly slow, and someone will file it as a bug.

RES_SLOT_MAX goes 4096 -> 16384 and RES_DOMAIN_MAX 32 -> 64, sized against recorded peaks -- but `g_directory` stays a STATIC array of atomic pointers (128 KiB). Making it growable is a publication-scheme change, deliberately deferred to Stage C. If Model B (a domain per kind) plus overlapping level domains plus a shared-immutable domain blow 64 domains, the fix is a redesign, not a constant bump.

The two-pass loose-over-pack walk means a CORRUPT loose file still shadows a good packed copy: it opens, then fails mid-read, which is a hard failure that (correctly) does not fall through to a lower mount. That preserves the existing open-failure-vs-read-failure semantics, and it is also a foot-gun for a user who drops a truncated mod file over a shipped asset. Stated, not fixed.
