name: SQUAD SOA-PLANES: placement is a plane destination, not an allocator object graph
thesis: Every owned thing in the manager -- registry identity, publication descriptors, payloads, conditioned resources, telemetry -- is a PLANE SET (N grain-aligned parallel arrays carved from one acquisition), and placement is a pure table lookup from (kind, lifetime, role, operation, destination) to a plane destination {root, arena, cell}; the five allocator models therefore become five ~40-line DATA literals (a root axis + an arena roster + a routing plane) over one allocation funnel, and Stage C's cache-striped ticket/consume planes are the same primitive with a lane axis added.
consume_model:
THREE OPERATIONS, THREE CONTRACTS, NONE DISGUISED AS ANOTHER.

1. BORROW (ano_res_bytes + the typed views) -- unchanged. A counted immutable view, valid only inside an open ano_res_read scope. This is what the renderer, the mixer and the VM use.

2. DESTRUCTIVE TRANSFER (ano_res_release) -- the block leaves the REGISTRY but never leaves the MANAGER. This resolves the transfer/wink-out contradiction, which is a genuine contradiction and not a detail:

THE PROBLEM: a block that an external consumer frees with mi_free must NOT also be reclaimed by mi_heap_destroy at wink-out. Today ano_res_release hands out a raw pointer and the renderer calls ano_aligned_free (ano_GltfParser.c:200, anotest_resgfx.c:271/:310) -- invisible to accounting forever, and a double-free waiting for the first domain that retires with an outstanding transfer.

THE DECISION: RES_ARENA_TRANSFER is NEVER a child of a winkable root. It is a dedicated process-lived mi_heap (res_placement.transfer_heap) created at res_place_init and destroyed at res_place_shutdown. Transferred blocks are tracked in a transfer plane ({void *p; size_t bytes; uint16_t cell; uint32_t owner;}) and returned with exactly one call:

    int ano_res_transfer_free(void *data);   // the ONLY way a transferred block dies

ano_aligned_free of manager memory disappears from src/ and tests/ entirely (a grep gate enforces it). Accounting becomes symmetric, so "allocations == frees at shutdown" becomes an assertable oracle. It goes RED today, because res_registry_external_allocation (registry:242-253) charges requested/serving/allocations/transfers and is NEVER reversed -- allocations already exceeds frees by the count of every decoded texture ever produced. That function is DELETED along with the caller-owned decode path it existed to excuse.

ZERO-COPY IS A PLACEMENT PROPERTY, NOT A SIZE THRESHOLD. res_place_plan sets site.transferable from the ARENA the model chose. A model that gives consumable kinds (RES_KIND_IMAGE_DECODED, RES_KIND_AUDIO_PCM, RES_KIND_GRAPHICS_BINDING) a transfer arena gets zero-copy release at ANY size, including a 20-byte block. A model that pools everything pays the copy and the benchmark SEES it (releases_zero_copy / releases_copied / release_copy_bytes, attributed by kind). C.7's line -- "hidden pooled copy-out is not destructive zero-copy" -- becomes structurally true instead of aspirational. Today the 1 MiB threshold is duplicated at three call sites and pretends to be a caller fact.

3. RETIRE (ano_res_unload / ano_res_domain_retire) -- unchanged in shape. The reclaim step now branches exactly ONCE, model-agnostically:

    invalidate publication -> epoch-defer each block -> after the reader grace point:
        if (res_place_domain_winkable(lt))  res_place_domain_wink(lt);   // one mi_heap_destroy
        else  for each teardown record: res_place_free(&rec->site, rec->p, rec->bytes);

The teardown-record list exists ONLY for models whose root axis is not lifetime (needs_teardown_records), and it is allocated from THAT MODEL'S OWN METADATA arena. A/B/D pay for their own teardown bookkeeping; C/E pay nothing. Spec section 0 -- "teardown bookkeeping lives in the hierarchy being measured" -- becomes a structural fact rather than a promise. It also deletes today's double walk: finish_domains_locked (registry:434-448) currently retail-frees every block AND THEN calls multipool_destroy + mi_heap_destroy.

DECODED PIXELS BECOME MANAGER-OWNED.
stb_image has no per-call allocator hook, but it HAS the STBI_MALLOC/STBI_REALLOC/STBI_FREE compile-time hooks. In src/resources/graphics/res_gfx_image.c we define them to route through a TU-local owner-thread staging pointer -- stb then allocates INTO the domain's staging arena. Decode -> copy ONCE into the planned home (RES_KIND_IMAGE_DECODED; RES_DEST_BULK or RES_DEST_EXTERNAL_TRANSFER, the model decides) -> res_account_copy charges that copy honestly -> adopt under the reserved derived key "<source>#rgba" with the encoded source's rid as a disclosed dependency.

    anores_t ano_resgfx_image_decoded(ano_res_lifetime, const ano_res_read *, anores_t src);
    anoresgfx_pixels ano_resgfx_pixels(const ano_res_read *, anores_t decoded); // counted view

The renderer either borrows the view under a read scope (the upload path) or takes an accounted ano_res_release followed by ano_res_transfer_free. The caller-owned stbi buffer is gone, and the public contract line "rgba; // caller-owned: free with ano_aligned_free" (anoptic_res_graphics.h:193) is deleted with it.

RENDERER RETAINS HANDLES.
parseGltf(VulkanContext *, const char *logical) is the last by-name asset route -- the renderer hands it a string and gets back a non-manager blueprint. It becomes handle-in / handle-out:

    anores_t ano_render_bind_scene(VulkanContext *, ano_res_lifetime, const ano_res_read *,
                                   anores_t scene);   // returns the "<src>#gpu" binding handle

ModelAsset / ModelMesh / ModelNode / ModelPrimitive (ano_GltfParser.h:20-51) are DELETED -- the whole calloc'd blueprint tree, which nothing in the tree ever frees (grep-verified: zero free sites; it leaks for the process lifetime, owned by no domain). flatten walks anoresgfx_scene views directly. The only two facts ModelAsset added over the scene view -- geometryPoolIndex (ano_GltfParser.c:146) and materialIndex (:241) -- become a typed derived resource: RES_KIND_GRAPHICS_BINDING, a three-plane block {geometry_pool_index | material_index | bindless_index} parallel to the scene's prims[], adopted under "<src>#gpu" with a dependency edge back to "<src>#gfx". The transient per-parse arrays (needed/srgb/loaded/bindless at :154-157, stagingBuffers at :180 -- plain calloc/free today) move into the TRANSIENT_IMPORT staging arena, so every byte the parse touches is accounted.

MEASURED REGRESSION, DISCLOSED NOT HIDDEN: retaining Sponza's scene block for the process pins a multi-MiB block that today is unloaded right after GPU upload (ano_GltfParser.c:423). Under Model A it pins the one multipool forever; under C/E it pins the level domain and dies at wink-out. That delta is exactly what the contest exists to judge, so it is counted and reported, never smoothed away.
streaming_pack:
All synchronous, owner-thread, correctness independent of Stage C (A.4 bullet 4).

OS SEAM (resources_os.h + _posix.c + _win64.c), three new ops:
    int rmos_read_at(rmos_file f, uint64_t off, void *buf, size_t cap, size_t *got);
    int rmos_advise(rmos_file f, uint64_t off, uint64_t len, rmos_advice a);
    int rmos_stat_hint(const char *abs, uint64_t *mtime, uint64_t *size);   // ADVISORY ONLY
CONTRACT, and it is the whole ballgame: *got == 0 is the ONLY EOF signal. 0 < *got < cap is a SHORT READ, not EOF -- 9P and SMB do this routinely -- and the CALLER loops. The loop is never folded into the seam, because its termination rule differs between "read to EOF" (res_read_all) and "deliver exactly len" (res_read_range). POSIX: pread(2), retry on EINTR. Win64: ReadFile with an OVERLAPPED carrying Offset/OffsetHigh on the ordinary synchronous handle -- positional and blocking, but per MSDN it ALSO advances the file pointer as a side effect, so one handle is single-owner in Stage A. That goes in the header, because someone WILL assume it is thread-safe and ship a data race.

CHUNK POOL (new src/resources/resources_stream.c) -- ano_mem_pool's FIRST production consumer, and its three consumers are real, not decorative:
    RES_ARENA_CHUNK = ano_mem_pool(block RMOS_CHUNK_MAX = 512 KiB, align ANO_CACHE_LINE,
                                   max_blocks 8), ano_mem_pool_reserve(2) at init.
    1. codec staging -- a compressed frame in, decode straight into the destination. A RAW
       ranged read needs NO chunk (rmos_read_at writes into dst directly); we do not fake a
       copy to justify the pool.
    2. res_hash_file -- fold FNV-1a-64 over a whole file 512 KiB at a time with no full-payload
       allocation (hot-reload confirmation, builder payload hashes, TOC hashing).
    3. the builder's streaming payload copy.
Exhaustion: ano_mem_pool_alloc returns NULL when capped-and-empty. The synchronous path degrades to one charged mi_malloc_aligned chunk -- never blocks, never fails a read. The test forces it with max_blocks=1 and two nested consumers.

    int res_read_range(const char *abs, uint64_t off, size_t len, void *dst);
      // 0 = exactly len; RES_RANGE_EOF = the file ended early (a caller error, not IO);
      // -1/-2 = open/IO failure, matching res_read_all's -1 "try the next root" vs -2 "stop"
      // convention that the mount walk already depends on. A silent short return is the exact
      // byte-truth violation A.2 forbids.
    int res_hash_file(const char *abs, uint64_t *hash, uint64_t *size);
    void *res_chunk_acquire(void); void res_chunk_release(void *);

DESTINATION PLANNING (kills the mandatory staging copy at registry:787-807, where every load allocates a default-heap buffer and then memcpy's it into the home):
    typedef struct res_sink {
        void *ctx;
        void *(*reserve)(void *ctx, size_t hint, size_t *out_cap);  // may hand back the HOME block
        void *(*grow)   (void *ctx, size_t need, size_t *out_cap);  // the hint LIED: charged spill
        void  (*commit) (void *ctx, size_t final);
    } res_sink;
    int res_read_sink(const res_sink *sink, const char *abs, size_t *out_size);
res_read_all becomes a thin wrapper. ano_res_get plans a home from the size hint and reads DIRECTLY into it; if the hint lied -- and it does lie on 9P/SMB, which is precisely why rmos_size_hint is documented as a hint -- grow() re-plans and charges exactly one hint_mismatch_copies. That copy is then a MEASURED property of the backend, which is the honest answer; "read directly into home" is only sound WITH a spill path, because a multipool class block cannot grow in place. Direct landing is gated on site.direct_landing, so a model whose payload arena cannot support it simply does not claim it. Public: ano_res_read_range(logical, off, len, dst) and ano_res_get_range(lifetime, logical, off, len), keyed through the reserved-key grammar.

CODECS (src/resources/codec/res_codec.{h,c}; no codec symbol escapes src/resources/, the same law that keeps cgltf and stb_image inside graphics/):
    RES_CODEC_RAW=0, LZ4=1, ZSTD=2, GDEFLATE=3 (a reserved byte, never implemented).
    external/lz4 vendored (lz4.c/h + lz4hc.c/h, BSD-2, four files; upstream ships no minimal
    CMake tree at that path, so a hand-written 6-line CMakeLists mirrors how jsmn/stb are
    pulled in at CMakeLists.txt:127-141). external/zstd behind option(ANOPTIC_ZSTD ... OFF).
Decode uses LZ4_decompress_safe ONLY -- never LZ4_decompress_fast, which on untrusted pack bytes is a remote memory-corruption primitive -- and requires the produced length to EQUAL dst_len exactly; anything else is corruption.
FRAMING: an entry payload is a sequence of {u32 csize_le}{csize bytes}, each decoding to exactly RES_CODEC_CHUNK = 496 KiB (last frame = remainder). 496, not 512: LZ4_COMPRESSBOUND(496K) is about 498 KiB and fits ONE 512 KiB pooled chunk; a 512 KiB frame bounds to ~514 KiB and does not. That arithmetic is the trap, and getting it wrong either silently grows the pool block or overruns the encoder.
Store-raw rule, a pure function of the bytes: csize >= size - size/64 -> store RAW.
EXPLICIT STAGE A NON-GOAL, stated so nobody ships a broken seek: ranged reads INTO an LZ4/zstd entry are unsupported (sequential frames carry no random-access index). Compressed entries are whole-entry loads; ranges work on RAW entries and loose files. Already-compressed assets (audio, PNG/JPEG) are stored RAW by builder policy, so the streaming shape is unaffected.

ANOPAK (include/anoptic_res_pack.h + src/resources/pack/res_pack.c): the frozen format from real.md section 9, serialized FIELD-WISE with the existing put16/put32/put64 helpers (resources_core.c:729-734) -- never fwrite a struct; padding is not portable, and the save frame already sets that precedent. 32-byte header {magic "ANOPAK\0\1", u32 entry_count, u32 flags, u64 toc_offset, u64 toc_hash}. 48-byte TOC entries sorted strictly ascending by rid: {rid, offset (4096-aligned), size (uncompressed), csize, codec, hash_id, entry_flags (bit0 = BAKED_SCENE), pad, payload_hash}.
DECISION RECORDED: payload_hash covers the UNCOMPRESSED bytes. It is then the same oracle the loose path computes, so packed-vs-loose equivalence is ONE hash, and it catches decoder bugs, which a stored-bytes hash cannot. Cost: verifying it requires decoding -- so payload verification is ALWAYS on in the builder, on by default in Debug, opt-in in Release (ANO_RES_PACK_VERIFY); the TOC hash is ALWAYS verified at mount.
MOUNT REFUSES, never half-mounts: bad magic/version; entry_count > 1<<20; toc bounds past EOF; toc_hash mismatch; rids not strictly ascending (a sort violation is corruption AND breaks bsearch); any offset not 4096-aligned; any offset+csize past EOF; ANY unknown or unavailable codec (a codec-2 entry in a build without zstd refuses the WHOLE MOUNT with one ERROR naming the codec -- it does not lazily fail at first read and it does not silently skip the entry; a pack you cannot fully read is a corrupt pack); any hash_id != 1.
NO mmap (SIGBUS over 9P/SMB). NO cached file handle: entry reads open a FRESH handle per read, which is resources_os.h:12's remote-FS floor doctrine. A squad WILL try to "optimize" that; it is rejected until Stage C has queue-depth evidence. The name table rides as an ordinary entry under the reserved logical "__anopak/names", so the format stays literally frozen while the builder still gets its collision check and the tests get enumeration. The TOC array is allocated through the placement seam with RES_ROLE_REGISTRY, so pack metadata is charged to the ownership hierarchy rather than to a stray mi_malloc.

LOOSE-OVER-PACK -- the ordering law, enforced STRUCTURALLY.
res_candidates (resources_core.c:230) returns ano_fspath only and cannot name a pack entry; worse, its table is write-root > mounts-newest-first > base, so a registered pack mount would SHADOW the loose base directory -- the exact INVERSE of the requirement.
    typedef struct res_source { res_source_kind kind; ano_fspath path;
                                const res_pack *pack; uint32_t entry; } res_source;
    int res_candidates_ex(const char *logical, size_t len, res_source *out, int cap);
TWO-PASS walk: pass 1 emits every DIRECTORY candidate (write root, dir mounts newest-first, base); pass 2 emits every PACK candidate (pack mounts newest-first). Loose-shadows-pack is then an INVARIANT OF THE WALK, not of registration order. res_candidates() survives unchanged as the DIR-only filter for ano_res_resolve/ano_res_exists (a packed asset resolves empty, by design). res_source_read_all is the single dispatch; ano_res_get's loop AND gfx_slurp (res_graphics.c:110-125 -- the silent trap: it calls res_candidates directly, so a packed .gltf's sibling .bin would fail to resolve while the .gltf itself loaded) both migrate onto it. Open-failure skips to the next root; a failure MID-READ is hard and does not fall through -- so a corrupt loose file still shadows a good packed copy, which is correct and matches res_graphics.c:121-122's existing rc == -1 vs rc != -1 logic.

BAKE. The conditioned block is already load-in-place shaped -- and that is exactly what makes it DANGEROUS. ano_resgfx_scene (res_graphics.c:607-650) bounds-checks array EXTENTS and validates NOTHING INSIDE THEM: prim.material indexes materials[], node.mesh indexes meshes[], node.parent/child_first/child_count index nodes[]/children[], children[i] and roots[i] index nodes[], indices[] index vertices[]. Those are safe today only because cgltf constructs them. The moment a block arrives from a pack, a truncated download, or a hostile mod, every one of them is an out-of-bounds read primitive handed straight to the renderer. THIS IS THE HIGHEST-SEVERITY ITEM IN THE LEDGER, and the plane-set framing fixes it once for every domain:
    int res_block_open(const void *bytes, size_t len, uint32_t magic, uint32_t version,
                       uint64_t layout_id, res_block_view *out);
validates magic, version, layout_id, block_hash (FNV-1a-64 over the block with the field zeroed), plane extents, and grain alignment. Each extension then runs ONE cross-reference pass at LOAD/ADOPT time -- never per-view, because a view is on the hot path. Any failure refuses the whole block: one log line, sentinel out.
Aliasing, resolved so nobody re-litigates it: the block is allocated storage with no declared type, so typed access through anoresgfx_vertex * is legal under C23 6.5p7, and 16/64-aligned offsets over a cache-line-aligned base satisfy alignment. The existing bare casts may stand.
    ano_resgfx_model asks the NAMESPACE FIRST: ano_res_get(lt, "<src>#gfx"). If the walk (pack or loose) has it -> gulp + validate + adopt into the same domain, same anores_t out, cgltf NEVER TOUCHED. Only on absence does it fall back to the cgltf ingest. A test-visible res_gfx_parse_count MUST read 0 after loading a baked scene -- the only honest oracle for "zero runtime parse work". Prose is not evidence.
Bake equivalence is a memcmp, not an interpretation: the builder emits exactly the bytes the cgltf ingest produces for that source. Deterministic because res_plane_layout is a pure function, the block is zero-filled before writing, and RES_PLANE_GRAIN is a compile-time constant. Dependency disclosure survives the bake: the deps array is rebuilt from the baked block's images[] at load, so prefetch behaves identically on both paths.

BUILDER (src/resources/pack/res_pack_build.c compiled into anoptic_core so the tests can drive it in-process; tools/anopak/main.c is a 40-line argv wrapper -- an offline tool may not reach into resources_internal.h from outside the module). Determinism is the merge bar, proven by building twice and memcmp'ing: a bytewise-sorted input walk (rmos_scan_dir order is explicitly unspecified, resources_os.h:83), entries in rid order, an rid COLLISION is a HARD error naming both paths (the runtime bsearch assumes uniqueness, and the registry's collision refusal fires too late), 4096-aligned explicitly zero-filled padding, codec choice a pure function of extension policy + the store-raw rule (never of mtime, locale, rand, or thread order), NO timestamps anywhere, saves/ EXCLUDED (user data under the write root is never packed -- the same exclusion the temp GC already makes). The builder decodes every entry back and checks payload_hash before the pack is renamed into place, and it writes the pack through res_write_protocol so a killed builder never leaves a half-pack that mounts.

INSTALL. A custom target runs anopak over the staged ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/resources tree after the shader/font/model staging steps; the bake runs in the same pass. main.c mounts the pack next to the existing ANO_DEV_RESOURCES dir mount (order is irrelevant to correctness, because loose-over-pack is enforced by the walk, not by registration order). STATED SO NOBODY "FIXES" IT: in a Debug/dev build the loose resources/ mount SHADOWS the pack BY DESIGN, so cgltf still runs and the zero-parse counter is nonzero. The zero-runtime-parse bar is measured on the INSTALLED tree ONLY. A squad chasing a green bench will try to weaken the shadowing; that destroys the invariant and is rejected.

HOT RELOAD (src/resources/resources_reload.c, dev-gated, frame-boundary, synchronous):
    int ano_res_reload_poll(anores_t *changed, int cap);
1. CANDIDATE (cheap, may lie): re-run res_candidates_ex and compare the WINNING SOURCE IDENTITY against the one res_bind recorded, plus rmos_stat_hint's (mtime, size) against the recorded pair. A newly-appeared LOOSE file over a PACKED entry is a candidate even though the row's source is the pack -- that is loose-over-pack hot reload through the one mechanism, and it is precisely the case a squad misses by only diffing mtime on the recorded path. 500 ms poll budget.
2. CONFIRM (authoritative): res_hash_file the candidate and compare to res_bind.content_hash. mtime LIES on 9P/SMB, so mtime is ONLY a filter; the content hash decides. This also kills the reverse false positive -- a touched-but-byte-identical file must NOT reload.
3. PUBLISH: load the new generation OUT OF PLACE into the SAME lifetime domain, validate fully (res_block_open + the cross-reference pass), then republish_locked():
       build the new immutable res_pub at generation+1
    -> ONE release-store OVER the old pub (NEVER NULL in between: ano_res_release's
       store-NULL-then-restore, registry:854-862, would let a reader acquire a sentinel for a
       fully live resource, breaking "old-complete or new-complete, never a spurious miss")
    -> queue_retire_locked(old pub, old block) at the bumped epoch
    -> collect_locked reclaims only after every reader lane registered before the swap is quiescent.
It REUSES the existing epoch machinery (queue_retire_locked:424, reader_safe:409, collect_locked:450). No second reclamation scheme appears.
GENERATION CONTRACT, and the renderer must be told: a reload BUMPS the generation, so handles held across a reload resolve to the sentinel and consumers re-get. The alternative -- stable generation, swapped data -- hands a reader a pointer whose bytes change underneath it.
DERIVED CASCADE, the thing that otherwise makes hot reload LIE: res_dependency_meta records parent -> children (a scene declares the images it needs); the reverse edge (source -> derived) exists nowhere. Without it, reloading models/x.gltf leaves the stale conditioned "models/x.gltf#gfx" scene published and serving old geometry forever -- hot reload that appears to work and is lying. On a confirmed reload of rid R, scan bound slots for any whose dep list contains R and cascade (retire; consumers re-get and re-ingest). A bounded scan at a 500 ms poll is free; a reverse index is a Stage C optimization, not a Stage A requirement. The same cascade is how a loose-over-packed-bake flip propagates.

wounds: THE ROUTING TABLE IS NOT 100% DATA. Model B wants a per-kind arena roster and D/E want a size split; both are data, but a real Model B will eventually want a plan_hook for a decision the table cannot phrase. The 'five models are pure data' claim is ~90% true, and plan_hook is the 10% where a contestant could smuggle in an advantage. Mitigation is a POLICY (a hook may only NARROW a site the table already produced; it cannot invent a root), enforced by review, not by the compiler. A policy is weaker than an invariant and we are not pretending otherwise.

NON-WINKABLE MODELS PAY A TAX THAT WINKABLE ONES DO NOT, AND WE CHOSE THAT ON PURPOSE. A/B/D need a per-domain teardown-record list to retail-free at retire; C/E need none. We charge the list to the model's own METADATA arena, which is the correct reading of spec section 0 -- but it is a consequence of OUR retire design, not purely of their hierarchy. A different registry could have kept one global block list and charged nobody. A rival squad could fairly argue this tilts the metadata column toward C/E by a few bytes per resource. It must be reported as a separate line item so a judge can subtract it.

CHUNKED IDENTITY PLANES ADD ONE INDIRECTION TO THE HOTTEST READ. ano_res_bytes goes from g_directory[slot] to plane_dir[slot>>12][slot&4095]. Both loads hit stable hot memory and the win is real (a growable directory instead of a 4096-slot freeze that Sponza's ~70 images plus derived siblings plus audio plus scripts plus an overlapping second level domain will approach), but it IS a cost on the read path that Stage C freezes. If the bench says it costs, the fallback is a flat 16384-slot static directory (128 KiB) and we eat the memory. We have not measured it, and we will not claim we have.

RES_PLANE_GRAIN IS 64, NOT ANO_CACHE_LINE. A baked block's interior plane offsets are an ABI and cannot vary with the target's cache line, which is 128 on arm64/Apple. So on a 128-byte-line machine, plane bases inside a conditioned block are 64-aligned, not cache-line aligned: the bake ABI beats the cache line. The affected platform is exactly the one we cannot run (macOS, recorded UNRUN). We are shipping a knowingly suboptimal layout on a platform we cannot measure, and saying so out loud.

ano_mem_stripe IS A NEW ALLOCATOR ON THE CRITICAL PATH WITH NO PRIOR ART IN THIS TREE. Its Stage A production consumers -- reader lanes, telemetry cell planes, audio PCM channel planes, the GPU binding table -- are genuine but modest. The big SoA payoff (Stage C's ticket/consume planes) is a bet on a transport that is not written yet. If Stage C comes out differently, stripe's Stage A justification thins toward 'an allocator with a bench and two small consumers' -- precisely the charge the spec levels at ano_mem_pool today. We take that risk deliberately, because Models D and E are INEXPRESSIBLE without it and the spec forbids omitting a promised tool.

ONE BLOCK FRAMING FOR EVERY DOMAIN MEANS ONE BUG IS EVERY DOMAIN'S BUG. res_block_open validates scene, PCM, bytecode, level, font bake and binding table. A defect in its extent math or its hash is simultaneously a graphics, audio, script, world and text vulnerability. The upside -- ONE hostile-input battery, ONE bake path, ONE pack entry type -- is exactly why we chose it; the downside is concentrated blast radius, and it makes res_block_open the single most fuzz-worthy function in the module.

THE TRANSFER HEAP IS PROCESS-LIVED, SO IT IS THE ONE THING A LEVEL WINK-OUT DOES NOT RECLAIM. A consumer that takes a destructive transfer and never calls ano_res_transfer_free leaks past domain retirement and no wink-out catches it. We make it visible (a transfer plane with a live count, and an ERROR at shutdown naming every outstanding block) but we cannot make it impossible without a reference count, which the spec forbids. This is the honest residue of resolving the transfer/wink-out contradiction: our answer moves the failure from 'silent double-free hazard' to 'loud, accounted leak'. It does not delete it.

RETAINING SPONZA'S SCENE HANDLE FOR THE PROCESS IS A MEASURABLE FOOTPRINT REGRESSION. A.2 requires consumers to retain manager views, and today the scene is unloaded right after GPU upload. Under Model A that pins a multi-MiB block in the one multipool forever. We count and report it rather than hiding it -- but the engine's steady-state RSS goes UP in Stage A, before the contest can fix it, and someone will read that as a regression rather than as the placement wound it is.

res_site IS 40+ BYTES AND IT LIVES IN EVERY res_owned_block, versus today's single `bool pooled`. On a corpus of thousands of tiny audio clips that is not trivial. We could pack it to ~16 bytes by recomputing serving and alignment from the arena spec at free time -- but recomputing is exactly the serving_size() sin we are deleting, and it is how a model's retuned config silently diverges from its accounting. We chose to store the truth and pay for it, and we are naming the price rather than burying it.

THE RESERVED-KEY GRAMMAR NARROWS THE PUBLIC NAMESPACE. res_path_validate will refuse '#' and '@' in a logical path. That is what makes derived-key type confusion structurally impossible, but it is a breaking narrowing: a user mod or asset pack with '#' in a filename becomes unloadable. The corpus has none today. We are announcing it rather than discovering it in a bug report.

EVERYTHING IN STAGE A IS OWNER-THREAD SYNCHRONOUS, SO LEVEL OPEN SERIALIZES glTF INGEST + IMAGE DECODE + WAV DECODE + SCRIPT COMPILE ON THE INIT THREAD -- a multi-second hitch on Sponza. That is CORRECT for Stage A (A.4 bullet 4: finish the synchronous reference first) and it MUST NOT be 'fixed' by sneaking a loader thread in ahead of Stage C: res_owned_alloc, ano_res_get, ano_res_domain_open and res_registry_adopt are all owner-thread-gated, and mi_heap_destroy is single-thread-owner, so a helpful worker thread corrupts mimalloc. We record the cold-open wall time as the baseline the ticket system will later have to beat, and we accept that the engine feels worse before it feels better.

include/anoptic_text.h + src/text/text.c + tests/anotest_text.c -- ano_text_font_load and ano_text_font_load_lit DELETED; FT_New_Face disappears from src/ entirely; anotest_text.c migrated to ano_res_init + ano_res_get + a HELD read scope + ano_text_font_load_memory (FreeType BORROWS the blob, so an early read_end presents as a FreeType crash, not a resource error -- ASan/TSan runs are mandatory after this change). The glyph bake becomes the '<font>#bake' derived resource; text_raster.c stops owning a private textHeap and its three logical paths come from config, not #defines.

include/anoptic_filesystem.h + src/filesystem/filesystem_{win64,linux,macos}.c + tests/templates/scratch.h -- ano_fs_chdir_gamepath DELETED, but ONLY after scratch.h is rebased on ano_fs_gamepath() + absolute joins in its own green commit: 15 test binaries call scratch_anchor_to_exe, and doing this in the wrong order reddens the LOGGER suite, where the failure looks like a resource bug.

tests/ NEW -- anotest_resplace.c (the routing oracle: every model x every plan -> the expected arena; alignment refusal; teardown-record accounting), anotest_resrange.c, anotest_rescodec.c, anotest_respack.c, anotest_resbake.c (the HOSTILE BLOCK BATTERY under ASan -- this is why res_block_open exists), anotest_reshotreload.c, anotest_resaudio.c, anotest_resscript.c, anotest_reslevel.c (>= 20 open/retire cycles with live_bytes, live_blocks, chunk_bytes, domains_live, retired_pending and stalled_readers all returning to the pre-open baseline), anotest_resremote.c + tests/shim/rmos_hostile.c (the deterministic half of the 9P floor: dishonest size, 1-byte short reads, delayed visibility, sync failure, sharing violation).

tests/ MODIFIED -- anotest_resgfx.c (skinned+animated handcrafted .gltf, handcrafted GLB, embedded data: image, bake round-trip byte equality, manager-owned-pixel retirement; and check_scene()'s `if (failures) return;` at :132-134, which silently skips EVERY per-field ground-truth assertion once any earlier check failed, is REMOVED -- the file's headline claim is currently defeatable by an unrelated failure). anotest_resources.c + anotest_resfault.c (the #ifndef _WIN32 temp-litter blind spot replaced with rmos_scan_dir, on the very platform whose ReplaceFileW/MoveFileExW rename path is likeliest to strand a temp). anotest_resownership.c (the race is 99.6% stale-sentinel today and its oracle is near-vacuous: rotate >= 8 live resources with a dwell window, assert a coverage floor so a regression to vacuity FAILS, and add reader-vs-retire and reader-vs-collect). anotest_resdurability.c (crash INSIDE ano_res_save_commit_ex -- neither fault harness drives a save today, and the stated reason is obsolete). anotest_resbench.c (res_registry_stats() sets direct_bytes = live_bytes = pools.live_bytes, so its 'chunks +N, direct +N' split compares a number against itself; that number must not be carried into the contest).

tests/CMakeLists.txt -- LABELS "resource" on every resource target so `ctest -L resource` becomes the matrix unit; per-sanitizer TIMEOUT scaling (anotest_resownership does 1.2M reads in 0.12s untraced, and under TSan the existing 120s ceiling is at risk -- scale iterations via argv rather than discovering a flake).

tools/verify/run_matrix.{sh,bat} + tools/verify/smoke_engine.py + tools/verify/floor_9p.sh + docs/resourcemgr/verification-matrix.md -- per cell: command, commit sha, platform, date, pass/fail, raw log path. The Sponza smoke asserts POSITIVE evidence (mesh/vertex/index counts above a floor), because render_api.c:154 logs a missing Sponza at ANO_WARN and continues: a grep for ERROR/FATAL would go GREEN on an engine rendering an empty room. The 9P cell must place BOTH the read roots and the write roots on the mount, or it is a normal Linux run wearing a costume. macOS recorded UNRUN, never claimed green.

docs/benchmarks/<stamp>-<scenario>.json -- raw machine-readable samples plus the full attribution-cube snapshot before and after each phase, plus the exact repro command, commit, build, platform, corpus and run count. A disabled benchmark plus prose is not evidence.
